models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.description: Anthropic's highest-capability model for difficult engineering, agent planning, and synthesis-heavy work.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4
models.description: Balanced frontier model with strong code generation, review quality, and dependable day-to-day agent behavior.

models.name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
models.description: Mature, widely deployed coding model with strong editing quality and stable tool use.

models.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
models.description: Fast lower-cost model for lightweight coding, triage, and support tasks.

models.name: GPT-4.1
models.description: General-purpose flagship with strong coding reliability, broad tooling support, and large context handling.

models.name: GPT-4o
models.description: Fast multimodal model that works well for interactive coding, review, and mixed text-image workflows.

models.name: o3
models.description: Reasoning-first model optimized for difficult analysis, planning, and evaluation-heavy workflows.

models.name: o3-mini
models.description: Smaller reasoning model aimed at structured problem solving with better cost efficiency.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
models.description: Large-context multimodal model suited to repo analysis, documentation synthesis, and cross-modal review.

models.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
models.description: Lower-latency Gemini model for review, routing, and lightweight coding assistance.

models.name: DeepSeek V3
models.description: Open-weight leaning coding and reasoning model with strong value for self-hosted or cost-sensitive workflows.

models.name: DeepSeek R1
models.description: Reasoning-focused DeepSeek model that performs well for structured analysis and agent evaluation loops.

models.name: Grok 3
models.description: High-end general model with strong realtime search-adjacent workflows and broad reasoning coverage.

models.name: GLM-4 Plus
models.description: General-purpose model with solid multilingual reasoning and decent cost efficiency.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.description: Terminal-first coding harness with patching, planning, and repo-aware execution loops.

harnesses.name: Codex CLI
harnesses.description: OpenAI command-line coding harness with strong editing, repo inspection, and explanation loops.

harnesses.name: Gemini CLI
harnesses.description: Google CLI harness optimized for long-context review, multimodal tasks, and scripted analysis.

harnesses.name: OpenCode
harnesses.description: Community-built coding environment with model switching, MCP support, and hackable workflows.

harnesses.name: OpenClaw
harnesses.description: Composable runtime for autonomous agents with reusable skills, orchestration, and evaluation loops.

harnesses.name: Cursor
harnesses.description: Popular AI-first IDE with inline edits, chat, and project-wide coding workflows.

harnesses.name: Windsurf
harnesses.description: IDE-style agentic coding environment optimized for iterative implementation and review.

harnesses.name: Zed
harnesses.description: High-performance editor with collaborative workflows and fast AI-assisted coding integrations.

harnesses.name: Continue
harnesses.description: Open-source assistant extension with custom model routing and self-hosted deployment options.

harnesses.name: Aider
harnesses.description: Git-aware terminal coding assistant focused on patching, commit-friendly edits, and flexible model support.

harnesses.name: Goose
harnesses.description: Open agent shell aimed at local execution, MCP tooling, and extensible workflows.

combos.name: Claude Opus 4 + Claude Code
combos.description: Claude Opus 4 with Claude Code. A strong implementation stack for shipping features, fixing bugs, and handling multi-file edits with solid repo awareness.
combos.notes: Best when edit quality and fast implementation loops matter more than exhaustive deliberation. Secondary fit: autonomous, research.
cons: Premium pricing
Best results skew toward premium hosted models

combos.name: Claude Opus 4 + Codex CLI
combos.description: Claude Opus 4 with Codex CLI. A strong implementation stack for shipping features, fixing bugs, and handling multi-file edits with solid repo awareness.
combos.notes: Best when edit quality and fast implementation loops matter more than exhaustive deliberation. Secondary fit: research, autonomous.
cons: Premium pricing

combos.name: Claude Opus 4 + Gemini CLI
combos.description: Claude Opus 4 with Gemini CLI. A good fit for codebase reading, specification review, and synthesis-heavy analysis across large contexts.

cons: Developer mindshare is lower
Not aimed at fully local workflows

combos.name: GLM-4 Plus + Gemini CLI
combos.description: GLM-4 Plus with Gemini CLI. A good fit for codebase reading, specification review, and synthesis-heavy analysis across large contexts.
combos.notes: Shines on long-context reading, repo summarization, and cross-checking large sets of files or docs. Secondary fit: coding, autonomous.
pros: Very good for docs and large-context review
cons: Developer mindshare is lower
CLI polish still trails the most mature tools

combos.name: GLM-4 Plus + OpenCode
combos.description: GLM-4 Plus with OpenCode. A practical local-first setup for developers who want portability, model control, and customizable tooling.
combos.notes: Appeals to teams that want open routing, self-hosting, or less dependency on a single hosted IDE workflow. Secondary fit: budget, coding.
cons: Developer mindshare is lower
Smaller ecosystem than major IDEs

usecases.name: Coding
usecases.description: Implementation-heavy software work where edit quality, repo navigation, and debugging matter most.

usecases.name: Research & Analysis
usecases.description: Long-context reading, synthesis, and evaluation across code, docs, screenshots, and specifications.

usecases.name: Autonomous Agents
usecases.description: Multi-step workflows that depend on planning, tool orchestration, and reliable agent loops.

usecases.name: Local-First
usecases.description: Portable or self-hosted stacks that favor openness, customization, and developer-controlled execution.

usecases.name: Budget
usecases.description: Best-value combinations that keep quality high without paying for the most expensive frontier stack.
